final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial food photo of a Japanese ramen close-up, rich tonkotsu broth, styled for appetite-driven food photography.

Style/medium: photorealistic food photography, macro editorial style in the spirit of Kenji Lopez-Alt or Saveur magazine spreads, shallow depth of field
Composition: tight overhead-to-45-degree close-up angle, 85mm macro lens, subject fills the frame with chopsticks lifting noodles as the focal hero
Subject: steaming bowl of tonkotsu ramen topped with chashu pork slices, soft-boiled ramen egg (溏心蛋) halved to reveal jammy yolk, nori sheet, sliced green onions; chopsticks mid-lift with a tangle of noodles, steam wisps rising
Scene/backdrop: dark matte wooden table surface, background softly blurred (bokeh), minimal props
Lighting/mood: warm directional side light with subtle fill, golden and inviting, emphasizing steam and broth sheen, deeply appetizing
Color palette: rich amber-gold broth as dominant tone, ivory noodles and creamy egg yolk as mid-tones, dark nori and charred chashu edges as contrast anchors, green onion as fresh accent
Materials/textures: glossy broth surface with visible fat droplets, tender chashu with caramelized crust, silky noodle strands, matte ceramic bowl rim
Avoid: flat lighting, overhead-only flat lay, plastic-looking food surfaces, over-saturated color grading, watermark, blurry noodles
